markers.reasoning: The car is using only 41.2% of its available grip on average. The remaining 59% is untapped performance. This typically means: braking is not hard enough, cornering is too conservative, or there is too much coasting between inputs.
markers.action: Focus on always being on either brake or throttle — never coasting. Brake harder initially (aim for 90% of max), trail-brake deeper, and apply throttle earlier on exit.

markers.finding: Only 75% of straight at full throttle (expected >89% for 387m straight).
markers.reasoning: On a 387m straight entered at 148 km/h, the car spent 25% of the distance with partial throttle, losing potential acceleration time.
markers.action: Get on full throttle earlier after corner exit and hold it longer before the braking zone. Only lift for gear changes or traction issues.

markers.finding: Only 88% of straight at full throttle (expected >90% for 943m straight).
markers.reasoning: On a 943m straight entered at 148 km/h, the car spent 12% of the distance with partial throttle, losing potential acceleration time.
markers.action: Get on full throttle earlier after corner exit and hold it longer before the braking zone. Only lift for gear changes or traction issues.

markers.finding: 1 wheel lockup event(s) in braking zone.
markers.reasoning: A locked wheel generates less braking force than a wheel at the slip limit (~10% slip). Each lockup extends the braking distance by 2-5 meters and flat-spots the tire, reducing grip for the rest of the session.
markers.action: Reduce initial brake pressure by 5-10% and apply the brake more progressively. The first 50ms of brake application should not be at maximum pressure. Squeeze the brake — do not stab it.

markers.finding: Only 76% of straight at full throttle (expected >84% for 321m straight).
markers.reasoning: On a 321m straight entered at 142 km/h, the car spent 24% of the distance with partial throttle, losing potential acceleration time.
markers.action: Get on full throttle earlier after corner exit and hold it longer before the braking zone. Only lift for gear changes or traction issues.

markers.finding: Rear wheelspin detected on corner exit.
markers.reasoning: The rear tires exceeded their traction limit during acceleration. Spinning tires generate less forward force than rolling tires, so the car actually accelerates slower despite more throttle input. It also risks snap oversteer.
markers.action: Apply throttle more progressively on exit. Wait until the steering is unwinding before increasing throttle past 50%. Think: 'unwind the wheel, then add throttle' — not both at once.
